question_text:
A student carries out four experiments:
1. Ice melting in warm water
2. Magnesium burning in oxygen
3. Candle wax melting near a flame
4. Rusting of an iron nail left in damp air

Which experiment shows an irreversible chemical change?
explanation: Experiments 2 (magnesium burning) and 4 (rusting) are irreversible chemical changes because new substances are formed and cannot easily be turned back into the original materials. Experiments 1 and 3 are reversible physical changes—melting can be reversed by freezing. To identify irreversible changes, ask: has a completely new substance been made that would be difficult to reverse?
